meta.context-ordering-rule: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- do not reinterpret the direction of this relationship
meta.context-compression-warning: acceptable context compression from your current context window in no way guarantees context fidelity in future context -- do not assume that what survives compression now will survive the next compression -- write durable state to files, not to context
meta.role: single-goal session continuity schema -- not a governance schema -- use when the work has one clear goal and no rules, ownership, or cross-scope coordination needed
meta.intent: any agent picking up this project can reconstruct what the goal is, what has been done, and what is known to go wrong -- humans are the source of missing truth when needed
meta.self-contained: this schema is the sole context source for authoring its own AGENTS files -- do not consult external operating procedures, modes of action, or supplementary governance docs -- if something required for authoring is not declared here, ask the human rather than reaching outside this file
meta.human-reported-issue-governance: bug reports and feature requests are not governed by this schema -- they are owned by whatever system the project declares

exit-to-governance.role: this schema has a natural ceiling -- replace it with AGENTS-schema-governance.json when any of the following emerge
conditions: rules or ownership decisions need to be recorded and enforced
multiple agents or teams need to coordinate without re-explaining context
scope grows to include distinct deliverables with different recipients
the same pitfall is rediscovered 3 or more times -- that is a rule waiting to be written
migration-protocol: read AGENTS-schema-governance.json fully before modifying any existing 1project files
the goal field in AGENTS-hello.md becomes the scope-statement in AGENTS-Vision.md -- do not discard, reframe as what this scope achieves
project-state-when-work-starts-and-stops does not map to agent-session-open-close-measurement -- ask the human to configure measurement commands for the governance schema's measurement-profile
existing AGENTS-Terms.md carries over unchanged -- it was optional in 1project but is required in governance, so it must exist before migration is complete
existing AGENTS-Pitfalls.md carries over unchanged -- add tier-history and inherits fields required by governance
freeform-patterns.not-working entries that triggered the exit should become candidate rules in AGENTS-Rules.md -- present to human for confirmation before writing
freeform-patterns.working entries should become candidate observed-good-patterns in the governance complexity-density block -- present to human for confirmation
narrative-ref carries over unchanged if present
author AGENTS-Vision.md and AGENTS-Rules.md as new files -- these do not exist in 1project and cannot be derived from existing files alone -- blocked-until human-confirms
update AGENTS.md schema-path from AGENTS-schema-1project.json to AGENTS-schema-governance.json
update AGENTS.md file-list to include all governance-required files
do not delete the original 1project schema file until the human confirms the migration is complete

meta.schema-family: AGENTS-schema-1project.json is a member of the AGENTS-schema-* family -- AGENTS-schema-governance.json is the full governance schema -- AGENTS-schema-narrative.json covers evolving narrative records for entities with multiple parties -- AGENTS-schema-gov2gov-migration.json governs migrations from one governance system into another
meta.sibling-isolation: if another AGENTS-schema-*.json file is present in the context window, do not merge, inherit, or cross-reference fields between schemas unless an explicit pointer field (narrative-ref, project-ref) declares the relationship -- each schema governs its own artifact set independently -- shared field names across schemas are structurally similar but operationally independent -- do not assume one schema's field definitions apply to another

types.string: single text value -- ask-if-ambiguous when meaning is organizational or contextual
types.file-path: relative path from workspace root to a file
types.enum: single value selected from a defined values list
types.verbatim: fixed string that must be copied exactly -- no paraphrase, no interpretation
types.term[]: array of entries in format: term [role: definition] -- not: excluded meaning
types.pitfall[]: array of failure records -- each declares: trigger (condition that caused it), failure-mode (what broke), resolution (confirmed correct behavior), session-count (times rediscovered -- higher means higher load priority) -- optional: superseded-by (FR/BR reference when picked up by external issue system), awaiting-external (blocked on a vendor or third-party relationship outside this project)

definitions.human-confirms: a human must explicitly approve before proceeding -- agent may not self-approve

AGENTS.md.role: entry point only -- contains no domain truth, no rules, no terms

generates.prompt: ask human to confirm workspace identity and state the goal in one sentence before writing anything

schema-path.role: path to AGENTS-schema-1project.json that governs this file set

context-ordering-rule.value: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- do not reinterpret the direction of this relationship

file-list.role: files present in this project -- AGENTS-hello.md always included -- optional files added when authored

AGENTS-hello.md.role: goal declaration and project-state-when-work-starts-and-stops -- the single source of what this project is for and what happened last work period

generates.requires-before-generate: AGENTS.md must exist and be valid
generates.prompt: ask human to state the goal in one sentence -- confirm external-tracking location if one exists

goal.role: the single declared goal of this project -- one sentence -- set once at authoring time -- do not change without human-confirms -- if the goal has shifted, that is an exit-to-governance signal

external-tracking.role: where bug reports and feature requests are tracked for this project -- free field, no tool assumption -- omit if no external system exists yet

project-state-when-work-starts-and-stops.role: open and close state for the current session -- agent reads open at session start, writes close at session end

open.role: state of the project at session start -- what exists, what is in progress

close.role: state of the project at session end -- what was completed, what remains

incomplete-session.role: path to HANDOFF.md left at the present working directory when a session ended before work was complete and no plan file was started -- absent means no incomplete session -- remove this field and the HANDOFF.md when the human confirms closure

narrative-ref.role: reference to a narrative record that tracks this project -- points to the record id in AGENTS-schema-narrative.json register -- omit if no narrative record exists

freeform-patterns.role: lightweight pattern notes -- what is working and what is not -- less rigid than governance observed-patterns, no signal mapping -- add when the project has accumulated enough sessions to notice repeating behavior

working.role: freeform notes on what has been going well -- no required structure -- omit if nothing notable

not-working.role: freeform notes on what has been going wrong or is frustrating -- no required structure -- if the same note appears across 3 sessions, that is an exit-to-governance signal

AGENTS-Terms.md.role: project-specific vocabulary -- add when a term is used in a way that differs from common meaning or needs to stay consistent across sessions

generates.trigger: human requests or agent identifies a term needing definition
generates.requires-before-generate: AGENTS-hello.md must exist and be valid

definitions.role: terms specific to this project -- do not define common words unless this project uses them in a non-standard way

AGENTS-Pitfalls.md.role: failure memory -- add when something went wrong so it is not rediscovered next session -- if the same pitfall reaches session-count 3, that is an exit-to-governance signal

generates.trigger: agent encounters a failure worth recording
generates.requires-before-generate: AGENTS-hello.md must exist and be valid

pitfalls.role: ordered list of failure records -- sort by session-count descending so highest-recurrence failures load first

trigger.role: condition or action that caused the failure

session-count.role: number of sessions this was rediscovered -- at 3, evaluate exit-to-governance

superseded-by.role: FR or BR reference number when this pitfall has been picked up by the external issue system -- omit if not yet filed

awaiting-external.role: blocked on a vendor, partner, or third-party relationship outside this project -- record who and why -- omit if not applicable

authoring-sequence.role: ordered steps for authoring AGENTS files from this schema -- follow in sequence -- do not skip blocked-until gates
steps: read this schema fully before writing anything
ask human to state the goal in one sentence and confirm workspace root
author AGENTS-hello.md -- populate goal, leave project-state-when-work-starts-and-stops open field for session start -- blocked-until human-confirms
author AGENTS-Terms.md only if the human identifies terms needing definition -- otherwise omit
author AGENTS-Pitfalls.md only if a failure has already been identified -- otherwise omit
update AGENTS.md file-list to reflect which optional files were authored
declare authoring complete -- do not add files not declared in this schema

schema.role: canonical starting-point templates for each file -- use as the initial content when authoring -- replace placeholder values with real data -- do not add fields not declared in this schema
schema.encoding: flat labeled key-value markdown -- nested objects indented two spaces -- arrays as hyphenated bullet lists -- no frontmatter markers, no code fences -- field names must match schema declarations exactly -- format must remain human-editable without tooling

AGENTS.md.context-ordering-rule: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- do not reinterpret the direction of this relationship
